Literature Gap / Motivation: Several studies compare models and focus strongly on predictive accuracy, but the project needed to show improvement against a clear baseline.
What We Implemented: We compared Seasonal Naive Baseline, SARIMAX Basic, KPI-based forecasting, and Hybrid SARIMAX + Random Forest.
Evidence / Result: Baseline MAPE improved from 22.48% to 16.20% using the Hybrid SARIMAX + RF model.
Why It Matters: Shows that the selected approach improved forecasting performance instead of only reporting one final model.

Literature Gap / Motivation: Many ML/DL studies report strong accuracy but can be difficult for restaurant managers to understand and apply.
What We Implemented: We used SARIMAX as the interpretable statistical core and added Random Forest only as a residual correction layer.
Evidence / Result: SARIMAX Basic achieved 18.50% MAPE, while the Hybrid SARIMAX + RF improved to 16.20% MAPE.
Why It Matters: Balances explanation and accuracy, which is important for managerial trust and academic defense.

Literature Gap / Motivation: The literature highlights the importance of temporal patterns, holidays, weather, previous demand, and operational context.
What We Implemented: We engineered calendar, seasonality, weather, event, lag, rolling average, and KPI-related variables.
Evidence / Result: The modeling dataset contains 474 daily observations and 50 modeling variables; the enhanced KPI layer contains 115 candidate engineered variables.
Why It Matters: Shows that model improvement was supported by structured feature engineering, not random model selection.

Literature Gap / Motivation: Food-service forecasting studies emphasize preprocessing, missing values, outliers, aggregation, and data quality.
What We Implemented: We transformed cleaned POS records into daily modeling, backtesting, and dashboard-ready datasets.
Evidence / Result: The pipeline moved from 276,109 cleaned POS records and 81 source columns to a 474-row, 50-column modeling dataset.
Why It Matters: Makes the analytical process transparent and defensible.

Literature Gap / Motivation: Many restaurant forecasting studies stop at model metrics and do not fully translate results into daily management decisions.
What We Implemented: We connected forecast outputs with staffing readiness, menu preparation, inventory planning, promotional timing, and operational risk.
Evidence / Result: The Executive Decision Dashboard presents KPIs, annual behavior, final holdout forecast, and operational value interpretation.
Why It Matters: Turns forecasting into a business decision-support tool, not only a technical modeling exercise.

Literature Gap / Motivation: A single holdout test can be limited because it may not show model behavior across different months or seasons.
What We Implemented: We included final holdout evaluation and annual rolling backtesting.
Evidence / Result: Rolling validation covers October 2025 to April 2026, while the final holdout window shows the strongest model comparison period.
Why It Matters: Strengthens the credibility of the results and avoids relying only on one final forecast window.

Literature Gap / Motivation: Most reviewed studies remain academic or experimental and do not provide a simple stakeholder-facing deployment layer.
What We Implemented: We deployed the dashboard through Streamlit and connected it to a stable public URL and QR code.
Evidence / Result: The project is accessible through the public dashboard link without changing the QR code when the app is updated.
Why It Matters: Improves communication with the professor, jurors, classmates, and restaurant stakeholders.

Literature Gap / Motivation: Point forecasts do not communicate uncertainty, which is important for operational planning.
What We Implemented: We will add a conformal prediction layer using historical validation residuals to produce lower and upper forecast bounds.
Evidence / Result: Expected output: point forecast plus prediction interval, for example $3,800 expected sales with a range such as $3,200 to $4,400.
Why It Matters: Helps managers plan staffing, inventory, and preparation under uncertainty.
